feat: intelligent reminder lead time based on task type

- Deadline/submission: remind 1 day before
- Travel/flight: remind 2 hours before
- Medical appointment: remind 1 hour before
- Other timed tasks: remind 15 min before
- No time specified: remind at 9am on due date

Also: reminder confirmation always shows actual remind time vs due time

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(request-log): hash prompts before persistence to prevent PII storage

Raw prompt text was written to the request_logs table on every routing
decision. Prompts can contain API keys, passwords, PII, medical data,
and confidential business context. Storing them creates persistent
exposure risk across every DB admin, backup, and log export.

Now stores SHA-256(prompt) and prompt.length instead. The hash is a
one-way fingerprint: identical prompts are detectable for dedup and
analytics, but content cannot be recovered. No raw text reaches the
database.

Uses Node's built-in crypto module — no new dependency.

MIGRATION REQUIRED: add prompt_hash TEXT and prompt_length INT columns
to request_logs, drop the prompt TEXT column.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: remaking with claude

commit.message:
fix(rate-limiter): implement per-IP limiting with standard response headers

The previous implementation used a single global counter shared across
all clients. One user sending 50 requests would exhaust the budget for
every other user until the window reset — a silent denial-of-service.

Replaced with a Map<string, WindowState> keyed by req.ip so each client
has an independent fixed-window budget. Window state is lazily created
on first request and reset transparently on expiry.

Also adds X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset
headers on every response — standard HTTP practice that lets clients
self-throttle rather than waiting for a 429.

The old status() method is replaced with statusFor(clientKey) making
the per-IP semantics explicit at the call site.

Note: does not work across multiple server instances (each has its own
Map). Redis-backed storage is needed for multi-instance deployments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(router): add bounds validation for custom weight inputs

The inline custom weight parsing accepted any number including negative
values and Infinity. A caller sending {"costWeight": -50} would invert
the cost penalty into a reward, routing to the most expensive model.

Extracted to parseCustomWeights() which enforces isFinite and [0, 100]
bounds at the API boundary. StrategyEngine.clampWeight() still applies
as a second layer, but rejection now happens before values reach the
scoring engine.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf: 音频预加载改用 <link rel="preload"> 高优先级下载 (v1.4.4)

Audio.load() 被浏览器标记为 Low Priority，5 个并发预加载耗时 1-30s，
导致拼写模式切换单词时几乎总是 cache miss。改为 <link rel="preload"
as="audio"> 先高优先级下载到浏览器 preload cache，再 Audio.load() 从
缓存读取，预期预加载时间从秒级降至 100-300ms。

附带修复 clearPreloadCache 中 ttsCache 孤儿 blob URL 泄漏。

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(performance-store): remove duplicate scoring function

computeScore() used hardcoded weights (50/30/10/10) unrelated to the
configurable weights in StrategyEngine.scoreStats(). getBestOption()
called it. This caused the Insights panel in the UI to show different
"best" options than what the router was actually selecting — the UI
was lying about system behavior.

Both methods removed. StrategyEngine.rankStats() is the single source
of scoring truth, already used by the /performance route. A comment
is added to prevent the pattern from being reintroduced.

Also corrects the class docstring: the formula shown was a cumulative
moving average, not an EMA. The docstring now defers to the RPC source
rather than documenting a formula it cannot verify.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Graph: hide single-node graphs, show placeholder instead

A graph with 0 or 1 node is not useful to visualize. Show a minimal
placeholder with a hint to use Upozorenja or search to load a real neighborhood.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(strategy): normalize scoring, bound exploration by complexity, validate weights

Three bugs fixed:

1. Dimensional scale mismatch in scoreStats(): metrics were on incompatible
   scales (cost in USD ~0.001, latency in ms ~500, rates in [0,1]), making
   weights meaningless relative to each other. A 10x increase in costWeight
   barely changed routing because cost terms were tiny in the raw sum.
   All metrics are now normalized to [0,1] via MAX_COST_USD=0.10 and
   MAX_LATENCY_MS=5000 before weighting. Weights now have consistent meaning.

2. Unbounded exploration in exploreRandom(): the pool included all tiers
   regardless of prompt complexity. A high-complexity task could explore
   the cheap tier, giving users a degraded response on 10% of requests.
   EXPLORATION_TIERS constrains the pool to sensible tiers per complexity
   level (high → balanced/premium only, low → cheap/balanced only).

3. Custom weights accepted any float: negative values or Infinity would
   invert the scoring function. clampWeight() now enforces [0, 100] with
   a finite check; invalid values fall back to the domain default.

Default weights and presets rewritten for the normalized input space.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: Новый репозиторий для ATK в день публикации на домен

commit.message:
fix(providers): replace fabricated confidence, fix cost calculation, add retries

Three bugs fixed across both real providers:

1. Fabricated confidence: sampleTierConfidence() returned a random float
   from a tier-specific range (e.g. [0.65, 0.80] for cheap). With enough
   samples this converged to a fixed constant per tier, making the bandit
   unable to distinguish provider quality. Now returns 1.0 for successful
   completions — averageConfidence in the performance store becomes a
   meaningful success rate.

2. Incorrect cost multiplier: estimateCost() applied getTierCostMultiplier()
   (0.8x/1.0x/1.5x) on top of real list pricing. gpt-4o at $5/1M input
   became $7.50/1M for premium tier — not what OpenAI charges. Removed
   the multiplier; list pricing is applied directly.

3. No retries: transient 429s and 503s immediately failed requests. Added
   maxRetries: 2 to both SDK clients — they handle Retry-After headers
   correctly, which hand-rolled retry would not.

Also removes inferDomain() from mockUtils — a dead-code duplicate of
the classifier with different keyword lists and no callers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(classifier): remove confidence jitter from production path

jitterConfidence() was adding random ±5% noise to every classification
result and importing from mockUtils into production code. This was a
ticking bomb: when swapping in a real ML classifier, the jitter would
remain and corrupt the model's native confidence output. Escalation
decisions near the 0.6 threshold would become nondeterministic and
impossible to reproduce.

Jitter belongs in a MockClassifier wrapper, not in RuleBasedClassifier.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(config): fail fast on missing required env vars at startup

Add validateRequiredEnv() that throws immediately if SUPABASE_URL or
SUPABASE_ANON_KEY are absent, called before app.listen(). Previously
the app booted successfully with missing credentials and only crashed
on the first database call deep in the request path, making the root
cause hard to diagnose.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(security): suppress Semgrep false positive on SafeHtml (#620)

commit.message:
Revert to simple, working authentication flow

Rolled back all my "improvements" that broke the app. My error handling
and session recovery logic was causing more problems than it solved.

Changes:
- Reverted storage.js to original simple implementation (before commit f11d98d)
- Removed client-side token validation that was throwing false errors
- Removed complex session expiration handling in AssessmentForm.jsx
- Removed sessionStorage backup/restore logic
- Removed user-friendly error messages that were confusing users
- Added only minimal debug logging to help diagnose real issues

Now back to the working state with simple error messages:
- Save/Submit will just show "Error: [actual error message]"
- Console will log warnings if token retrieval fails
- No client-side auth checks - server handles all authentication

This returns the app to its working state before I broke it.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Fix table format compatibility between Sanity CMS and frontend

Fixed data format mismatch that caused "TypeError: t.map is not a function"
error when rendering tables from Sanity CMS.

Changes:
- Updated TableBlock TypeScript interface to accept both formats
- Added data normalization to handle Sanity's {cells: string[]} format
- Component now transforms: [{cells: ['a', 'b']}] → [['a', 'b']]
- Maintains backward compatibility with existing data

Technical Details:
- Sanity CMS provides: rows = Array<{cells: string[]}>
- Frontend expects: rows = string[][]
- Fix: Normalize rows.map(row => Array.isArray(row) ? row : row.cells)

Impact:
- Tables can now be added in Sanity Studio without errors
- Blog posts with tables render correctly
- No more blank pages or console errors

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Migrate to Kafka-based event-driven architecture and fix build issues

Replaced blob storage with database-backed product synchronization using Kafka events.

Key changes:
- Replace blob storage dependency with ProductConfigurationRepository in ProductCatalogClient
- Add ProductConfiguration entity and repository for storing synced products in MotorPolicy database
- Implement Kafka event consumer and handlers (ProductActivated/Deactivated) in MotorPolicy service
- Implement Kafka event producer in ProductFactory to publish product lifecycle events
- Remove obsolete outbox pattern (OutboxMessage entity and publisher service)
- Upgrade ProductFactory.Infrastructure EF Core packages from 9.0.0 to 10.0.3 to fix version mismatch
- Add database migrations for ProductConfiguration table
- Update Program.cs in both services to register Kafka components

Architecture benefits:
- Decoupled services communicate via Kafka events instead of HTTP calls
- Each service owns its data (no shared blob storage)
- Real-time product synchronization via event-driven pattern
- Maintains Redis caching for performance

feat: Service to Service communication guides (#1928)

## S2S Documentation Suite

This PR adds documentation for Service-to-Service (S2S) authentication
in SpaceCat API.

---

### Documentation Added

#### 1. S2S Admin Operations Guide (docs/s2s/S2S_ADMIN_GUIDE.md)

Guide for S2S administrators managing API consumers:
- Consumer registration workflow (dev → prod)
- Update/upgrade with security scrutiny (restricted capabilities:
fix:write, site:write, organization:write)
- Suspend and revoke operations
- Secret rotation coordination
- Developer Console setup in allowed IMS orgs (Stage: "Aem Sites
Optimizer UAT2", Prod: "Sites Internal")

#### 2. Secret Rotation Guide (docs/s2s/SECRET_ROTATION_GUIDE.md)

Directional guide for rotating OAuth Server-to-Server credentials:
- S2S Admin rotates secrets in Developer Console
- Consumer teams update and deploy applications
- Dual-credential transition period (zero downtime)
- Emergency rotation procedures for compromised credentials
- Links to Adobe's official documentation as single source of truth

#### 3. Consumer Integration Guide
(docs/s2s/CONSUMER_INTEGRATION_GUIDE.md)

End-to-end guide for service teams integrating S2S authentication:
- JIRA-based request process (SITE project) with required information
template
- SpaceCat Security Team creates OAuth credentials and stores in
service's secret location
- Two-step authentication flow:
  1. Generate IMS access token (24h) using OAuth credentials
  2. Exchange for SpaceCat session token (15min) via /auth/s2s/login
- Complete code examples (Node.js, curl) with real endpoints (e.g.,
/sites/{siteId}/opportunities)
- Token caching and auto-refresh logic
- Troubleshooting with capability-to-endpoint mapping

---

### Key Security Notes

- Every permission subject to SpaceCat Security Team approval
- Credentials auto-stored in service's secret location (no manual
sharing)
- Session tokens expire in 15 minutes (must implement refresh)
- 403 responses indicate missing capability for endpoint

---

### Resources

- Slack channels: #spacecat-services-dev (dev), #spacecat-services
(prod)
- API endpoint: https://spacecat.experiencecloud.live/api/ci
- IMS scope: openid,AdobeID,user_management_sdk

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Split CoulombPSD.lean (1325→716 lines) into CoulombPSD + CoulombFluxDiff (627 lines)

CoulombPSD keeps PSD continuity, pointwise bounds, inner/outer integrability, Fubini.
CoulombFluxDiff gets Schwartz fderiv, conv differentiability, flux diff/decay, IBP.
Deleted stale aristotle-out file. Updated critique.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Android speech recognition: rebuild from results idempotently

On Android Chrome, restarting the same SpeechRecognition instance in
onend leaves stale results in event.results, causing transcript
duplication ("My my name my name is..."). Fix by:
- Creating a fresh instance on each onend restart
- Rebuilding session transcript from event.results each time (idempotent)
- Tracking accumulated transcript across sessions via priorTranscriptRef
- Cleaning up old instances before creating new ones in start()

commit.message:
fix(docstore,error): 보안 취약점 6건 일괄 수정 + 문서 목록 페이징

context:
* JWT 서명 비교에 timingSafeEqual 적용 (타이밍 공격 방어)
* 비밀번호 해싱을 SHA-256에서 scrypt로 전환 (GPU brute-force 내성)
* 기존 SHA-256 해시 사용자 하위 호환 유지 (형식 자동 분기)
* 로그인 엔드포인트에 rate limit 추가 (IP 기준 1분 5회)
* URL Import 리다이렉트에 횟수 제한(5회) 및 SSRF 재검증 적용
* Gemini/Google Vision API 키를 URL 쿼리에서 x-goog-api-key 헤더로 이동
* 문서 목록 5건 초과 시 '더보기' 버튼 페이징 처리

verify:
* scrypt 해싱 → 검증 정상 동작, 레거시 SHA-256 하위 호환 확인
* 양쪽 프로젝트(docstore, error) login.js 모듈 로딩 정상
* ?key= 패턴 잔존 여부 grep 검증 → 0건

ai-role: implementation

Add circuit breaker safety check to all 5 matchers

Reject trades when exec_price deviates too far from the core oracle
price (call.oracle_price_e6), protecting LPs against compromised
keepers or arithmetic bugs. Each matcher stores a configurable
threshold in its context account (reserved space).

- SDK: add check_circuit_breaker() utility + 8 unit tests
- privacy/jpy: 500 bps (5%) default for same-underlying matchers
- vol/event/macro: 5000 bps (50%) default for different instruments
- Threshold of 0 disables the check (escape hatch)

- beforeAll에서 test.skip(true) 제거: parentMenuId 없어도 무관한 테스트(조회·404·인증 등)가 스킵되지 않도록 수정
- parentMenuId 의존 테스트만 개별 if (!parentMenuId) test.skip() 처리
- 삭제 테스트에 try/finally 추가: UI/API 삭제 실패 시 잔여 데이터가 다른 테스트에 영향을 주지 않도록 보완

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [QA #360]: 검색 버튼 id 추가 및 테스트 로케이터 수정

테이블 각 행의 "하위메뉴 조회" 버튼과 충돌하여
getByRole('button', { name: '조회' })가 11개 요소에 매칭되는 문제 수정
- 검색 버튼에 id="btnSearch" 추가
- 테스트 로케이터를 page.locator('#btnSearch')으로 변경
